message:
fix(ir): allow duplicate subgraph and cluster keys instead of merging

The IR builder previously deduplicated subgraphs and clusters by key,
merging members into a single group when the same key appeared more than
once. This is incorrect for both Mermaid and DOT, where repeated
subgraph declarations create distinct groups that happen to share a key.

- Remove `cluster_index_by_key` and `subgraph_index_by_key` dedup maps
  from `IrBuilder`; every `push_cluster` / `push_subgraph` call now
  always allocates a new entry.
- Rename `find_subgraph_by_key` to `first_subgraph_by_key` and add
  `subgraphs_by_key` for exhaustive lookup.
- Update existing core tests and timeline subgraph lookup in the Mermaid
  parser to use the new API.
- Add parser-level regression tests for duplicate keys in both DOT
  (`duplicate_dot_subgraph_keys_remain_distinct_groups`) and Mermaid
  (`flowchart_duplicate_subgraph_keys_remain_distinct_groups`).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
